title: SB 1374 allows schools, apartments, and farms to consume their self-generated solar energy and save on utility costs

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "SB 1374 restores the ability for utility customers with on-site solar generation to be credited for self-consuming that electricity.",
    "The bill ensures that utility customers with multiple meters receive the same compensation for self-consumed energy as single-family homeowners.",
    "California cannot meet its carbon emission reduction goals without properly supporting over 10,000 school sites in the transition to clean energy.",
    "Solar energy and battery storage are critical to protecting learning time in schools and meeting California’s clean energy and climate adaptation goals.",
    "The lack of support for self-consumption of solar energy in public schools can divert funds away from educational resources and programs.",
    "SB 1374 requires the CPUC to update tariffs to treat multiple-metered customers equitably in terms of self-consumption.",
    "The bill aims to restore the ability for various entities like schools, community colleges, and farms to save on energy costs through solar and energy storage."
  ]
}
```
